name: Project.Commander.Command.Feature_BsToolSignalForwarding
observations: Fixed sequential processing continuation after BsTool execution by implementing signal forwarding from BsToolCommandService to CommandQueue.
Root cause: BsToolWorker submitted directly to thread pool bypassed CommandQueue.start_processing(), missing automatic signal forwarding that CommandWorker (FBC/RPC) receives.

Solution: Added _handle_worker_completed() method that forwards command_completed signal to CommandQueue, enabling NodeTreePresenter.handle_command_completed() to fire and continue sequential workflow.
Integration: Part of unified queue architecture ensuring FBC/RPC/LOG commands execute sequentially with proper continuation logic.

name: Project.Commander.Command.Method_HandleWorkerCompleted
observations: CRITICAL signal forwarding method in BsToolCommandService that bridges BsToolWorker and CommandQueue for sequential processing.
Implementation: Receives command_completed from BsToolWorker, forwards to CommandQueue via self.command_queue.command_completed.emit(command, result, success, token).
Without this method: Sequential processing stops after first BsTool execution because NodeTreePresenter never receives completion signal.
Backward compatibility: Also emits legacy bstool_execution_completed signal for UI components not yet migrated to queue architecture.
Debug logging: Added explicit log '_handle_worker_completed: Forwarding signal to CommandQueue' for troubleshooting signal flow issues.

name: Project.Commander.Command.Pattern_QRunnableSignalBridging
observations: Architectural pattern for bridging QRunnable workers with PyQt signal systems when workers bypass normal processing queues.
Problem: QRunnable workers submitted directly to QThreadPool don't automatically integrate with parent queue's signal forwarding mechanisms.
Solution: Service layer (BsToolCommandService) acts as signal bridge by connecting to worker signals (_handle_worker_completed) and re-emitting on parent queue's signals.
Signal flow: Worker.signals → Service.handler → Queue.signals → Presenter.handler, creating complete signal chain despite direct thread pool submission.
Alternative considered: Route BsToolWorker through CommandQueue.start_processing() like CommandWorker, rejected due to different execution model (synchronous subprocess vs async telnet).
Applicable to: Any QRunnable worker that needs to participate in sequential processing workflows but can't use standard queue submission patterns.
